summary: Recent updates to the Canvas area of Firefox have focused on enhancing the efficiency and reliability of WebGL, a crucial technology used for rendering graphics in web applications. Notably, improvements include adopting modern memory management practices with `std::make_unique` and `MakeRefPtr`, which help ensure safer and more efficient handling of resources. Additionally, the cleanup of transform feedback preparation and updates to indexing caches boost performance in graphics rendering tasks. These changes help developers create smoother and more responsive visual experiences in web applications relying on the Canvas and WebGL technologies, ultimately leading to **better graphics and smoother interactions for users**.
